name:
EASTER SUNDAY
THE RESURRECTION OF THE LORD

lectionary.gospelTitle: Mary Magdalene Finds the Tomb Empty (Jn 20:1-9)
lectionary.thematicTag: Easter Sunday — The Resurrection of the Lord

readings.summary: Peter recounts the deeds of Jesus.

readings.citation:
Ps 118:1-2, 16-17, 22-23
This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad.
readings.antiphon: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad.

readings.summary: Set your heart on the greater gifts.
Mary Magdalene finds the tomb empty.

antiphons.text:
I have risen, and I am with you still, alleluia.
You have laid your hand upon me, alleluia.
Too wonderful for me, this knowledge, alleluia, alleluia.

planningNotes: Multiple Masses — outdoor (7:30a, outdoor Masses) and indoor (9:30a, 11:30a, 5:30p) configurations differ
Outdoor Gospel Acclamation: Halle, Halle, Halle (Marty Haugen) + lectionary verse
Indoor Gospel Acclamation: Hallelujah (Leonard Cohen, arr. Gary Denk & Dan Houze) + verse by Gary Denk
Outdoor Offertory: Hallelujah Is Our Song (Sarah Hart & Sarah Kroger)
Indoor Offertory: He Is Not Here (Derek Campbell, BPM 150)
Homily Meditation (11:30a only): You Say (Lauren Daigle, arr. Dan Galbraith, keys of F or G)
Sprinkling Rite: Lead Us to the Water (Tom Kendzia & Gary Daigle)
Meditation: What A Beautiful Name — Gospel Version (Hillsong, BPM 70)

massNotes: Welcome Litany: Easter Welcome Litany (All Are Welcome)
Sprinkling Rite: Lead Us to the Water (Tom Kendzia & Gary Daigle)
Gospel Acclamation — OUTDOOR (7:30a + outdoor Masses): Halle, Halle, Halle (Marty Haugen) + verse: 'Christ, our paschal lamb, has been sacrificed; let us then feast with joy in the Lord.'
Gospel Acclamation — INDOOR (9:30a, 11:30a, 5:30p): Hallelujah (Leonard Cohen, arr. Gary Denk & Dan Houze) + verse by Gary Denk: 'Christians praise the paschal victim...'
Homily Meditation (11:30a only): You Say (Lauren Daigle, arr. Dan Galbraith, BPM 74, charts in key of F or G)
Offertory — OUTDOOR: Hallelujah Is Our Song (Sarah Hart & Sarah Kroger)
Offertory — INDOOR: He Is Not Here (Derek Campbell, BPM 150)
Meditation: What A Beautiful Name — Gospel Version (Hillsong, BPM 70) — 'Death could not hold you...'
Eucharistic Acclamations from Mass of Glory (Bob Hurd, arr. Dan Houze) — Holy Holy BPM 120, We Proclaim Your Death BPM 110, Amen BPM 115

penitentialAct.title: Easter Welcome Litany (All Are Welcome)
penitentialAct.description: Sprinkling Rite: Lead Us to the Water (Tom Kendzia & Gary Daigle)

gloria.title: Glory to God (Mass of Joy & Peace)
